# Cloudflare Gotchas

> Known Cloudflare Workers and Pages patterns that cause deployment failures or unexpected behavior. Load all HIGH entries before deploying to Cloudflare.

## Entries

- [Hyperdrive proxy URLs are request-scoped -- never cache postgres connections](hyperdrive-connection-caching.md): Module-scope cached DB connections point to dead Hyperdrive proxy endpoints on subsequent requests, causing 500s. Create fresh connections per request. HIGH.
- [postgres.js max_lifetime creates stale connections with module-scope caching](postgres-max-lifetime-workers.md): max_lifetime: 1 tears down the connection but the cached instance is still reused. Combined with module-scope caching, subsequent requests get dead connections. HIGH.
- [@cloudflare/vite-plugin builds a Worker, not a Pages project](vite-plugin-worker-deploy.md): Deploy with `wrangler deploy` from `build/server/`, not `wrangler pages deploy build/client/`. Using Pages deploy gives static-only site with no SSR. MEDIUM.
- [postgres.js with fetch_types:false returns JSONB as strings](postgres-jsonb-as-strings.md): With fetch_types:false (required for Hyperdrive), JSONB columns arrive as raw strings, not objects. Use a parseRow() helper with typeof check. MEDIUM.
- [Unapplied migration files cause silent column-missing failures](unapplied-migration-silent-failure.md): Migration file in repo but never run against production. INSERTs fail with confusing "column does not exist" errors. Always verify migrations were applied. HIGH.
